rationale: A is better anchored for Kafka Connect 4.2.0: it names the exact runtime class, version-pinned docs, and concrete neighboring tests, and it explicitly marks uncertainty. B gets the behavior mostly right, but its doc anchor is too generic and its test guidance is non-specific, making it less useful for an engineer changing the Java code.

A: Does not explicitly name `AllowlistConnectorClientConfigOverridePolicyTest`, which is the most direct 4.2 neighboring test for the allowlist policy.
B: Uses an imprecise docs anchor (`https://kafka.apache.org/42/documentation/`) instead of the versioned Kafka Connect config/user-guide pages.
Does not name the key neighboring test classes or the exact policy classes in enough detail.
Relies on generic directory guidance where the question asked for concrete version-pinned anchors.
